jamul indian village is known for its rich cultural heritage and strong sense of community. the village is planning to build a new community center with a unique design. the center will be constructed in the shape of a regular hexagon, with each side measuring 30 meters. the community also decided to incorporate a circular garden in the center of the hexagon, with the garden's circumference touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides.

the area of the hexagonal community center can be calculated by using the formula for the area of a regular hexagon. a regular hexagon can be divided into six equilateral triangles, each with a side length of 30 meters. the area of one equilateral triangle is (sqrt(3)/4) * side^2. multiplying this by six gives the total area of the hexagon. this calculation will provide the area of the hexagonal community center.

the radius of the circular garden can be determined by finding the distance from the center of the hexagon to the midpoint of one of its sides. this distance is known as the apothem of the hexagon. the apothem can be calculated using the formula (s * sqrt(3))/2, where s is the side length of the hexagon. once the radius is found, the area of the circular garden can be calculated using the formula for the area of a circle, which is π * radius^2. this calculation will provide the area of the circular garden. the pen is mightier than sword
